method_atom: A dynamical-systems analysis of the stationary solutions of a driven nonlinear transport flow: locate and classify the critical (fixed) points of the flow equations, showing that only saddle points and centre-type points arise, and derive an upper bound on a conserved transport quantity. A time-dependent linear-perturbation study whose governing perturbation equation is shown to be invariant under the choice of any member of a family of driving potentials, used both to prove stability of the stationary solutions under generic outer boundary conditions against a radially propagating disturbance and to extract an effective wave-propagation (metric-like) geometry for the perturbations.
object_atom: Inviscid, rotational (isothermal and polytropic) multi-transonic accretion flow driven onto a Schwarzschild black hole through various pseudo-potentials, with the flow's angular momentum as the conserved quantity and the acoustic geometry seen by sound waves in the flow.
brief_reason: The critical-point classification, conserved-quantity bound, and potential-invariant perturbation/stability-plus-effective-geometry machinery carry across fields, while the transonic black-hole accretion flow driven by pseudo-potentials is the astrophysics-specific object.
